text: Upon entering the abdomen, the surgeon observes inflammatory adhesions surrounding a Meckel's diverticulum. The adhesions are carefully dissected using scissors and electrocautery, revealing the diverticulum.
The surgeon identifies an area of perforation in the small bowel, visually distinct from surrounding tissue.
The surgeon identifies and frees the base of the Meckel's diverticulum. An endoscopic stapler is carefully positioned around the base, ensuring the small bowel lumen remains unobstructed.
The surgeon frees the last attachment of the Meckel's diverticulum, completing its separation from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon places the resected specimen into an endo bag for removal from the abdominal cavity.
